title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 45 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across the UK.

festivals.name: Up Helly Aa
festivals.description: A spectacular fire festival in Shetland featuring torchlit processions and the burning of a viking galley, celebrating Norse heritage.

festivals.name: Burns Night
festivals.description: Celebration of Scottish poet Robert Burns with traditional suppers featuring haggis, whisky, and poetry readings across Scotland.

festivals.name: Chinese New Year Festival
festivals.description: Colourful celebration in London's West End with dragon parades, acrobatics, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Six Nations Rugby Championship
festivals.description: Major international rugby tournament featuring England, Scotland, Wales, and other nations with matches across the UK.

festivals.name: St David's Day
festivals.description: Welsh national day celebrating patron saint David with parades, concerts, and traditional Welsh celebrations throughout Wales.

festivals.name: Cheltenham Festival
festivals.description: Premier horse racing festival featuring four days of top-class steeplechase racing and colourful crowds in traditional attire.

festivals.name: Mother's Day
festivals.description: Family celebration honouring mothers with cards, gifts, and special meals across the UK.

festivals.name: Easter
festivals.description: Major Christian holiday featuring Easter egg hunts, church services, and family celebrations across the UK.

festivals.name: Shakespeare's Birthday Celebrations
festivals.description: Festival honouring William Shakespeare with processions, performances, and celebrations in his birthplace.

festivals.name: St George's Day
festivals.description: Celebration of England's patron saint with traditional festivities and red and white decorations throughout England.

festivals.name: Early May Bank Holiday
festivals.description: Public holiday marking early May with community events, fetes, and celebrations across the UK.

festivals.name: Hay Festival
festivals.description: World-renowned literature and ideas festival featuring authors, thinkers, and performers in a beautiful riverside setting.

festivals.name: Chelsea Flower Show
festivals.description: The world's most famous garden show featuring innovative garden designs and horticulture displays in central London.

festivals.name: Isle of Wight Festival
festivals.description: Multi-genre music festival attracting major international acts and thousands of festival-goers to the Isle of Wight.

festivals.name: Glastonbury Festival
festivals.description: Iconic music and arts festival set in a farm with diverse performances and a legendary reputation for culture and counterculture.

festivals.name: Trooping the Colour
festivals.description: Military ceremony celebrating the sovereign's official birthday with elaborate pageantry and horse guards parade in central London.

festivals.name: Henley Royal Regatta
festivals.description: Prestigious rowing event on the Thames attracting international competitors and spectators in traditional summer dress.

festivals.name: Royal Ascot
festivals.description: Premier horse racing event attended by royalty, featuring world-class racehorses and flamboyant fashion displays.

festivals.name: Edinburgh Festival
festivals.description: World's largest arts festival featuring theatre, comedy, music, and dance with hundreds of shows across the historic city.

festivals.name: Summer Bank Holiday
festivals.description: Late summer public holiday marking the end of summer with family outings and bank holiday celebrations across the UK.

festivals.name: Reading and Leeds Festival
festivals.description: Twin music festival hosting major rock, indie, and alternative acts with separate venues in Reading and Leeds.

festivals.name: Notting Hill Carnival
festivals.description: Vibrant multi-day street festival celebrating Caribbean culture with music, dance, food, and colourful parades.

festivals.name: Edinburgh Military Tattoo
festivals.description: Spectacular military display featuring pipe bands, marching troops, and fireworks on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle.

festivals.name: Brecon Beacons National Park Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Intimate jazz festival set in the scenic Brecon Beacons mountains featuring world-class jazz musicians.

festivals.name: Fringe Festival
festivals.description: Edinburgh's alternative festival showcasing emerging artists, theatre, and comedy alongside the main Festival.

festivals.name: Buskaid Festival
festivals.description: Street performance festival celebrating buskers and street musicians in Edinburgh during the Festival season.

festivals.name: Latitude Festival
festivals.description: Arts and music festival in Suffolk featuring thoughtful programming, theatre, literature, and diverse music acts.

festivals.name: Cambridge Folk Festival
festivals.description: Long-established folk music festival featuring traditional and contemporary folk artists in a beautiful riverside setting.

festivals.name: Wimbledon Championships
festivals.description: The world's oldest tennis tournament featuring elite players competing on grass courts in a tradition spanning 150 years.

festivals.name: T in the Park
festivals.description: Major Scottish music festival featuring diverse genres and attracting tens of thousands of festival-goers.

festivals.name: Green Man Festival
festivals.description: Independent music and arts festival in the Black Mountains featuring eclectic programming and beautiful natural setting.

festivals.name: Creamfields
festivals.description: Major electronic dance music festival set in Cheshire featuring world-renowned DJs and producers.

festivals.name: Secret Garden Party
festivals.description: Intimate and quirky multi-genre festival in Cambridgeshire known for its creative atmosphere and eclectic line-ups.

festivals.name: Leeds Festival
festivals.description: Major rock and alternative music festival attracting international headliners and thousands of young festival-goers.

festivals.name: Electric Picnic
festivals.description: Multi-genre festival celebrating music, theatre, and arts on the island with a creative and inclusive atmosphere.

festivals.name: Bestival
festivals.description: Eclectic music and arts festival on the Isle of Wight featuring themed days and diverse programming.

festivals.name: Great North Run
festivals.description: One of the UK's largest half marathons attracting thousands of runners from across the world to Newcastle.

festivals.name: London Fashion Week
festivals.description: Major fashion industry event showcasing collections from leading designers twice yearly in London.

festivals.name: Oktoberfest UK
festivals.description: German beer festival celebration with traditional food, beer, and entertainment across UK venues.

festivals.name: Halloween
festivals.description: Spooky celebration featuring fancy dress, pumpkins, and trick-or-treating across the UK.

festivals.name: Bonfire Night
festivals.description: Annual celebration with bonfires and fireworks commemorating the thwarted Gunpowder Plot with events across the UK.

festivals.name: Christmas Markets
festivals.description: Festive seasonal markets featuring crafts, gifts, and traditional food across major UK cities including London, Manchester, and Edinburgh.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrating Christmas with family gatherings, gift exchanges, and traditional festivities.

festivals.name: Boxing Day
festivals.description: Public holiday following Christmas featuring sales, sporting events, and the traditional Fox Hunt Day.
